question: When does a transaction implicitly BEGIN in Oracle?
options: With the first executable DML statement after connect or after a previous COMMIT/ROLLBACK

explanation.uz: Oracle'da tranzaksiya oshkormas boshlanadi — ulanishdan yoki oldingi COMMIT/ROLLBACK dan keyingi birinchi DML (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) bilan. Alohida BEGIN kerak emas.
explanation.en: A transaction starts implicitly with the first executable DML after connect or after a prior COMMIT/ROLLBACK. No explicit BEGIN is needed.

question: Which of these ENDS the current transaction?
options: COMMIT, ROLLBACK, or any DDL (which causes an implicit commit)

explanation.uz: Tranzaksiya COMMIT yoki ROLLBACK bilan tugaydi. Har qanday DDL (CREATE/ALTER/DROP/TRUNCATE) esa oshkormas COMMIT qiladi — ochiq tranzaksiyani ham saqlaydi.
explanation.en: A transaction ends with COMMIT or ROLLBACK. Any DDL (CREATE/ALTER/DROP/TRUNCATE) issues an implicit commit, ending the open transaction too.

question: Where does Oracle keep the OLD values of uncommitted changes (for rollback and read consistency)?

explanation.uz: O'zgartirishdan oldingi qiymatlar UNDO segmentlarida (undo tablespace) saqlanadi — ROLLBACK va boshqa sessiyalarga o'qish izchilligi (read consistency) uchun. Redo esa qayta bajarish uchun.
explanation.en: Pre-change values are stored in UNDO segments (undo tablespace) — used for ROLLBACK and for read consistency to other sessions. Redo is for re-applying changes.

question: Can another session see your UNCOMMITTED changes?
options: No — until you COMMIT, other sessions see the last committed (read-consistent) data
Only if it is the same user

explanation.uz: Yo'q. COMMIT qilinmaguncha boshqa sessiyalar UNDO orqali oxirgi COMMIT qilingan (izchil) ma'lumotni ko'radi. Oracle'da 'dirty read' yo'q.
explanation.en: No. Until you COMMIT, other sessions see the last committed (consistent) data via UNDO. Oracle has no dirty reads.

question: What does ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT sp_name do?
options: Undoes changes made after the savepoint, keeping earlier changes and the transaction still open
Commits everything up to the savepoint

explanation.uz: SAVEPOINT'gacha ROLLBACK faqat savepoint'dan keyingi o'zgarishlarni bekor qiladi; undan oldingilari saqlanadi va tranzaksiya ochiq qoladi. Yakuniy COMMIT/ROLLBACK hali kerak.
explanation.en: ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT undoes only changes after the savepoint; earlier changes stay and the transaction remains open. A final COMMIT/ROLLBACK is still needed.

question: A session terminates abnormally (lost connection) with an uncommitted transaction. What happens?
options: It is rolled back (PMON cleans it up)

explanation.uz: Sessiya g'ayritabiiy uzilsa, COMMIT qilinmagan tranzaksiya ROLLBACK qilinadi — buni PMON background jarayoni tozalaydi (loklarni bo'shatadi, undo qo'llaydi).
explanation.en: On abnormal disconnect, the uncommitted transaction is rolled back — PMON cleans it up (releases locks, applies undo).

question: Which isolation level gives a transaction a consistent snapshot for its whole duration, preventing non-repeatable reads and phantoms?

explanation.uz: SERIALIZABLE tranzaksiya boshlangan paytdagi izchil snapshot'ni butun davomida ko'radi — non-repeatable read va phantom'lar bo'lmaydi. READ COMMITTED esa har so'rovda yangi snapshot oladi.
explanation.en: SERIALIZABLE sees a consistent snapshot as of the transaction start for its whole duration — no non-repeatable reads or phantoms. READ COMMITTED takes a fresh snapshot per statement.

question: What is Oracle's DEFAULT transaction isolation level?

explanation.uz: Oracle'ning standart izolyatsiya darajasi — READ COMMITTED: har SELECT so'rovi so'rov boshlangan paytdagi izchil ma'lumotni ko'radi.
explanation.en: Oracle's default is READ COMMITTED: each query sees data consistent as of the moment that statement began.

question: What is an AUTONOMOUS transaction?
options: A transaction that auto-commits every statement
An independent child transaction (PRAGMA AUTONOMOUS_TRANSACTION) that commits or rolls back separately from the main transaction
A transaction that cannot be rolled back

explanation.uz: Autonomous transaction — PRAGMA AUTONOMOUS_TRANSACTION bilan e'lon qilinadigan mustaqil bola tranzaksiya. U asosiy tranzaksiyadan alohida COMMIT/ROLLBACK qiladi (masalan, xato bo'lsa ham log yozib qo'yish uchun).
explanation.en: An autonomous transaction (declared with PRAGMA AUTONOMOUS_TRANSACTION) commits/rolls back independently of the main transaction — e.g. to write a log even if the caller rolls back.

question: How many transactions can a single session run over its lifetime?
options: Many, one after another (only one active at a time)

explanation.uz: Bitta sessiya umri davomida ko'p tranzaksiya bajaradi — biri COMMIT/ROLLBACK bilan tugagach, keyingisi boshlanadi. Bir vaqtda faqat bitta faol tranzaksiya bo'ladi.
explanation.en: A session runs many transactions sequentially over its life — each ends with COMMIT/ROLLBACK, then the next begins. Only one is active at a time.
